children.heading: Civil forfeiture proceeding
children.label: a Civil forfeiture proceeding
children.text: Any fishing vessel (including its fishing gear, furniture, appurtenances, stores, and cargo) used, and any fish taken or retained, in any manner, in connection with or as a result of the commission of any act prohibited by shall be subject to forfeiture to the United States. All or part of such vessel may, and all such fish shall, be forfeited to the United States pursuant to a civil proceeding under this section.section 773e of this title

children.heading: United States district court jurisdiction
children.label: b United States district court jurisdiction
children.text: Any district court of the United States shall have jurisdiction, upon application by the Attorney General on behalf of the United States, to order any forfeiture authorized under subsection (a) and any action provided for under subsection (d).
the proceeds from the sale of forfeited property;
the remission or mitigation of forfeitures; and

children.heading: Bond or other security; disposal of seized fish
children.label: d Bond or other security; disposal of seized fish
A
children.text: stay the execution of such process; or

children.label: B
children.text: discharge any fish seized pursuant to such process;

Any fish seized pursuant to this subchapter may be disposed of pursuant to the order of a court of competent jurisdiction or, if perishable, in a manner prescribed by regulations of the Secretary or the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating.

children.heading: Presumption of violation
children.label: e Presumption of violation
children.text: For purposes of this section, it shall be a rebuttable presumption that all fish found on board a fishing vessel which is seized in connection with an act prohibited by were taken or retained in violation of the Convention and this subchapter.section 773e of this title
